definitions: To relieve oneself of a burden of worry or a secret.
To confess something that has been causing anxiety or guilt.
examples: Telling her the truth really got a weight off his mind.
I need to talk to you; I have something to say that will get a weight off my mind.
After the exam, it was a huge weight off her mind.
lemma: to get a weight off one's mind
synonyms: to make a clean breast of it
source: to get a weight off one's mind
